issue.title: ci: stale workflow not working

labels.name: repo-1b0c319b
labels.description: New issue waiting for initial maintainer triage

issue.body: ### Describe the bug It has been over a month (30 days) since we integrated [the stale workflow](https://github.com/npmx-dev/npmx.dev/blob/main/.github/workflows/stale.yml) in https://github.com/npmx-dev/npmx.dev/pull/2580. But in all issues and PRs where a `"stale"` label got added, this label got instantly removed again: This happens because we have two jobs that run `action/stale` and interfere with each other because the run in parallel: - the PR job has the config `days-before-issue-stale: -1` -> removing all `"stale"` labels from issues (which have just been added by the issue job) - the issue job has the config `days-before-pr-stale: -1` -> removing all `"stale"` labels from PRs (which have just been added by the PR job) ### Additional context _No response_ ### Logs ```shell-script ```

issue.title: fix: stale workflow
issue.body:
### 🔗 Linked issue

- Closes #2845 

### 🧭 Context

We want to automatically mark bug issues and PRs as `"stale"` after 30 days of inactivity and after 7 more days close them automatically. Read more in the original creation of this workflow: https://github.com/npmx-dev/npmx.dev/pull/2580

### 📚 Description

This PR fixes the issue by using two distinct labels for marking and closing.

comment.body: Blocked? Which moment in this process would be "blocked"? Also, if they fix it, we use it, but in the meantime, we do our own fix.

comment.body: @trueberryless you can't merge them since `only-issue-types: 'bug'` doesn't care about PRs. that's why we have 2

issue.title: fix: stale workflow
issue.body:
### 🔗 Linked issue

- Closes #2845 

### 🧭 Context

We want to automatically mark bug issues and PRs as `"stale"` after 30 days of inactivity and after 7 more days close them automatically. Read more in the original creation of this workflow: https://github.com/npmx-dev/npmx.dev/pull/2580

### 📚 Description

This PR fixes the issue by using two distinct labels for marking and closing.

comment.body:
@trueberryless I think the issue is with the tag names. Both "stale-pr-label" and "close-pr-label" have the same name, so the action can't distinguish between them.
We should just give them 2 different names

something like
```
stale-pr-label: 'stale'
close-pr-label: 'stale-to-close'
```

issue.title: Create evaluation framework
issue.body:
Currently, changes to the detection engine are validated mostly through existing tests, but there is no reliable way to measure whether a new version actually improves detection quality or introduces regressions.

### Goals

* Measure detection quality over time.
* Detect regressions before release.
* Compare old and new detection logic objectively.
* Reduce risk of false positives.

### Tasks

#### Create a benchmark dataset

* [ ] Collect a set of manually reviewed GitHub accounts.
* [ ] Label accounts into categories
* [ ] Store benchmark accounts in a versioned dataset.

#### Build evaluation tooling

* [ ] Create a script that runs the library against the benchmark dataset.
* [ ] Generate metrics
* [ ] Export results as JSON/Markdown for easy comparison.

#### Add regression comparison

* [ ] Create a way to compare results between:

  * Current release
  * Candidate release
* [ ] Highlight accounts whose classification changed.

### Notes

The primary focus should be minimizing false positives. Missing some automated accounts is generally less harmful than incorrectly classifying legitimate developers as automations.

 <template>

-      <ChartGlobalEventsEvolution :data="dataset" :timestamps :width :height />
+      <ChartGlobalEventsEvolution
comment.body:
Wouldn't it make more sense to move all this processing directly into the ChartGlobalEventsEvolution component, since it's the only one that uses it? It looks like an unnecessary abstraction that creates lots of props.
We could just pass "data," and the component does what it needs. Perhaps we can move some of it to composables to reduce the component's length too.
We can do it later, btw, just thinking.

issue.title: [AUTOMATION] Nexory
issue.body: ### GitHub Username Nexory ### GitHub User ID 18486706 ### Why do you believe this is an automated account? issue.title: Agent detection switch to @unveil/identity package
issue.body:
The voight-kampff-test package currently in use has been deprecated and has been left untouched for a few months. I would recommend using the [@unveil/identity](https://github.com/unveil-project/identity) package, which powers agentscan.tools and its action as well.
It has better detection and is more up to date.

comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -33,6 +33,14 @@ inputs:
     description: "Label to add when an account is classified as automated"
     required: false
     default: "agentscan:automated-account"
+  auto-close:
+    description: "Whether to automatically close issues/PRs for detected automations"
+    required: false
+    default: "false"
comment.body: auto-close should be disabled by default. Where do you read that needs to be true per the objective?

Maybe we can apply the AI commit multiplier only to amplify automation/spam-related flags: fork spikes, commit bursts, and PR spam patterns, rather than applying it globally.
Also, it could work better if we support multiple tiers (50%, 70%, 90%) with graduated multipliers, and handle cases where AI commits could multiply a flag, but there are no flags. what you think? 🤔

issue.title: [Replacement]: tsc

labels.name: repo-d746fd83
labels.description: Used to signal if a suggested replacement has been accepted.

issue.body:
### Package to replace

tsc

### Suggested replacement(s)

typescript

### Manifest type

preferred (lighter or more modern alternative package)

### Rationale

The tsc package is frequently mistaken for the tsc binary from the typescript package. While the author has added deprecation notices to the repository and included warnings when the package is used, the package itself has not been officially deprecated on npm. Officially deprecating it would help prevent accidental installations and protect users from potential security risks.

### Availability

_No response_

### Code example (optional)

```js

```

issue.title: Request to deprecate package
issue.body:
Hello there 👋 
I noticed that the README mentions this package has been deprecated, but it's still available on npm and continues to warn users that it's not the TypeScript compiler.

Given that many developers accidentally install this package when they want to compile a TypeScript file, I'm concerned it could pose a security risk if it falls into the wrong hands or is targeted by malicious actors.

Would you consider officially deprecating the package on npm and archiving the repository? This would help protect the community by making it clear that the package is no longer maintained.

Thank you for your time and for helping keep the ecosystem safe.

issue.title: Possible improvements to Identity Detection System
issue.body:
**Confidence scoring** - Return classification with a confidence level (0-100) based on number of corroborating signals. One flag = 40% confidence, three independent flags = 85%.

**PR merge rate analysis** - Calculate ratio of merged PRs to total PRs. Bots typically have low merge rates (spam rejected), while legitimate contributors have higher rates.

**Known bot whitelist** - Add list of legitimate automation accounts (dependabot, renovate, etc) that should not trigger certain flags, reducing false positives.

**Automation type classification** - Categorize as `legitimate_automation`, `likely_spam`, or `uncertain` instead of just binary automation detection.

**Temporal event degradation** - Weight recent events more heavily than old ones. Fork spam from 6 months ago matters less than recent fork spam. Use exponential decay on older events.

comment.body:
Thank you for reaching out! The burst detection currently doesn’t take PR status merged into account, because a rapid sequence of fork → branch creation → PR creation should still be flagged as potentially automated behavior.

That said, I’ll make sure to double-check that this pattern is actually associated with an open status PR before it gets flagged. Getting merged doesn't really matter, in this case.

issue.title: detect open + auto-close PR pattern
issue.body:
A lot of stats farmers often open multiple PRs that they immediately close.
This should be detectable in GitHub events.
Should make sure to check a high number, that repo is not owned by the user, that it's auto-close in a very short timeframe. Possibly with a fork + branch create pattern close to it.

issue.title: fix: limit chart data to last 90 days
issue.body: This limits the chart dataset to the last 90 days, to avoid long streaks of empty days when a set contains a very old date. | Before | After | |-|-| ||| ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Account events timeline now shows only the most recent 90 days of activity. * Timeline labels and chart data use hourly granularity within that 90-day window for clearer recent activity trends. * Events are filtered to active event types so the chart focuses on relevant activity.
